have if you look at the Ford rate curve

at 3.75% and the fed's most recent do
plot from September calls for a neutral
rate setting of of 3% so it's very
unlikely we see the neutral rate rate
move 75 basis points in three months
it's very unlikely um highly unlikely
it's almost impossible you know for them
to revise their neutr rate estimates
that high especially in the context of a

reiterated that they do not take into

parade but with respect to uh do you buy

research providers is you buy the dip
when you're in a risk-on market regime

year in performance Chase has nothing to
do with fast asymmetrically reaction
doish reaction function has nothing to

matters from a risk management

dirty it's a euphemism for what has
become a dirty word on on global Wall
Street which is Market timing risk
management the the the proper way to
risk manage your portfolio is to buy the
dip when you are in a risk-on market
regime and sell the rip when you are in
a riskof market regime and so to the
extent we remain in a risk on Market
regime you should be bullishly disposed
to asset markets it's that simple I mean
literally this is the whole point of our
systematic kiss portfolio construction

more sophisticated system that we use to
help our institutional clients stay on
the right side of markers they they
essentially the same system one just has
more options in terms of the factor long
short bets that that that Dr Mo signals
on and so with respect to that you know
all you have to do is just pay attention
to the evolution of those signals as
long as we remain in a risk on Market
regime then you just stay bullish you
stay you stay invested and you refresh
the model tomorrow or look at the
refresh of the model tomorrow and if
it's still bullish then you don't do
anything and if you refresh the model
the day after tomorrow and it's still
bullish you don't do anything and you
keep refreshing the model until one day
